author: anon-a7c6025f
content:
Analysis: CPU reductions are scalar loops (no SIMD). GPU sum does a full tensor download to CPU. GPU sum_axis has a PTX kernel but it's naive (1 thread/output, no warp reduction). mean/mean_dim/sum_dim always CPU-fallback on GPU tensors.

Plan:
A. Write a proper GPU parallel reduction kernel for full sum (tree reduction with shared memory + warp shuffle)
B. Fix GPU sum_f32 backend to use the new kernel instead of CPU fallback
C. Wire mean() to use GPU sum + scalar divide  
D. Wire sum_dim/mean_dim to use the existing sum_axis GPU kernel (currently bypassed)
E. CPU SIMD reductions can be a follow-up — GPU is the 500x bottleneck
